title: Verizon upgrades the 5G network for the Indy 500, promises speeds over 1.0Gbps at the track
description: The annual Indy 500 race is coming up – it’s happening this Sunday (May 24) and the legendary track expects massive crowds to gather to watch the racing live. Last year, around 350,000 people showed up at the track and shared a whopping 61TB of data over their mobile connections. This year, Verizon has spent months preparing the 5G infrastructure at the track and it has upgraded the radio hardware to deliver a 400% increase in bandwidth. The carrier thought things through to ensure a good experience from the start – since paper tickets are mostly a thing of the past, you need to load a...

title: Apple's new hardware chief orders another reshuffle to speed up product development
description: Apple is getting a new CEO later this year as Tim Cook will be succeeded by the Senior VP of Hardware Engineering, John Ternus. But in addition to the news of a new CEO, the company also announced a significant restructuring. Johny Srouji becomes the new Chief Hardware Officer and merges the hardware engineering and hardware technologies teams. Almost immediately after taking his new role, Johny Srouji commenced a big reshuffle. Kate Bergeron, for example, will pass on the main product design management to two of her deputies, while she will oversee product reliability. She's taking on...

title: Samsung is facing the biggest worker strike in its history over employee bonuses
description: Samsung’s memory division reported record sales for Q1, but this has caused discontent within the company, specifically the Device Solutions (DS) business. DS is home to the memory business, System LSI (which designs chipsets) and the foundry business (which fabs chips for Samsung and external clients). Now Samsung is facing the biggest strike in its history – with 48,000 employees threatening to walk out – which is due to start on Thursday, unless an agreement is reached or the government intervenes. The strike is planned to last 18 days. SK Hynix is also based in Korea and it is the...

title: AT&T will introduce a new fee for prepaid customers in June
description: If you are an AT&T customer using a prepaid plan, you need to know this – the carrier is introducing an “AT&T Administrative & Regulatory Cost Recovery Fee” to the tune of $2.63 per service payment. This change will come into effect on June 22 of this year. You can follow the Source link below to see a detailed explanation of what this fee is supposed to cover. AT&T’s postpaid accounts already had an Administrative & Regulatory Cost Recovery Fee, which is $3.99 per line per month, so the prepaid plans are cheaper. That postpaid fee was $3.49 per line until December of last year, by the...

title: Huawei reveals which chipset is powering the MatePad Pro Max
description: Huawei unveiled the MatePad Pro Max tablet earlier this month, and today it's taken the unusual step of revealing the chipset at its helm. Ever since the US sanctions started against the company seven years ago, Huawei has sometimes been very cagey about revealing which exact chips power its devices and we've come not to expect transparency on this front by default. Now at a HarmonyOS conference in China, the company just came out and revealed that the MatePad Pro Max is powered by the Kirin T93 Pro SoC. Unfortunately, Huawei still hasn't said anything about it other than the name. But...

title: iQOO 15T arrives with Dimensity 9500 Monster chip, 8,000mAh battery and 200MP main cam
description: The iQOO 15T arrives on the scene as the first T-series device from the brand. It gets a different chip compared to the regular iQOO 15 and Ultra models, replacing the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chip with MediaTek’s Dimensity 9500 “Monster Edition” SoC. iQOO 15T is equipped with MediaTek's Dimensity 9500 Monster Edition chip This is a custom version of the regular 9500 with improved frame-rate stability thanks to iQOO’s in-house Q3 gaming chip. The phone is configurable with up to 16GB LPDDR5X RAM and 1TB UFS 4.1 storage. The front is dominated by a 6.82-inch 8T LTPO AMOLED...

title: Android 17's 'Continue On' feature will let you move tasks between devices
description: Google made quite a few announcements on the first day of Google I/O 2026, and one of them is "Continue On," which is an Android 17 feature that app developers can implement to provide cross-device continuity for their users. In Google's own words, "Continue On enables users to start an Android app on one Android device and then transition to another device in their Android ecosystem, continuing the user journey they started." In simpler words, if you are working on a document in the Google Docs app on your Android smartphone, with Continue On, you continue the same work on an Android...

title: Apple Sports app expands to more than 90 new countries and regions, gets features for FIFA World Cup 2026
description: Apple has announced that the Apple Sports app, introduced in 2024, is now available to download from the App Store in more than 170 countries and regions, following its expansion into over 90 new markets. Apple is also making it easier for fans to follow the FIFA World Cup 2026, kicking off on June 11, by allowing them to explore tournament groupings and customize their scoreboards just by following the entire tournament or their favorite national teams in the Apple Sports app. When you follow a team in the Apple Sports app, Live Activities are enabled and shown on your iPhone's Lock...

title: CMF Phone 3 Pro tipped to arrive later than usual
description: Based on Nothing’s usual release cycle, the successor to the CMF Phone 2 Pro was expected to debut this month. However, a new leak suggests that the company may have delayed the launch of the CMF Phone 3 Pro by several months. As per a tipster, the CMF Phone 3 Pro is now expected to launch in the third quarter of the year, likely between August and September. Instead, the tipster claims that Nothing will launch a budget Nothing branded phone ahead of the CMF device. Last month, several key specifications of the CMF Phone 3 Pro surfaced online. The phone is rumored to be powered...

title: Wear OS 7 unveiled with widgets, Live Updates, improved battery life
description: At the Google I/O 2026 conference, the search giant unveiled Wear OS 7, the latest version of its smartwatch operating system based on Android 17. The update introduces several new features, including Widgets, Live Updates, and Gemini Intelligence on select smartwatches. Google also claimed that Wear OS 7 can deliver up to a 10 percent improvement in battery life for smartwatches upgrading from Wear OS 6. Wear OS 7 introduces Widgets, which Google says are designed to be flexible and dynamic. These widgets will be available in 2x1 and 2x2 layouts, similar to the widget formats...

title: Honor X7e 4G emerges in listing
description: An upcoming Honor 4G budget phone emerged in a listing shared by a Saudi Arabian retailer. The Honor X7e 4G is shown in its three official colors, which include black, orange and blue. The back of the device resembles the recently launched Honor Play 80 Plus, and the listing for the device reveals they share some key specs, including a 6.61-inch TFT LCD with HD+ resolution and a 7,500 mAh battery. Honor X7e 4G The X7e’s chipset is not disclosed but we do get confirmation that it features 2x Cortex-A75 cores clocked at 2.0GHz alongside 6x Cortex-A55 units at 1.7GHz. According to...

title: vivo S series reportedly making a comeback in India
description: vivo is reportedly planning to revive the S series in India. The vivo S1 was the last S series phone to launch in India back in 2019. The new phone is said to be called the vivo S2. According to the report, the vivo S2 will reportedly be more expensive than the mid-range pricing of its predecessor. The handset is said to be priced between INR 45,000 and INR 50,000, placing it in the premium segment. vivo S1 The vivo S2 is said to launch in July. While its specifications remain under wraps, the S series is expected to continue focusing on stylish, lightweight and premium design, as...

title: Realme 16T pricing leaks along with a live image
description: Realme is unveiling the 16T on May 22, and yesterday the brand shared some more details about the device ahead of the launch event. Today the pricing for India has been leaked alongside one live image showing the device itself and another one showing its box. According to a tipster over on X, the Realme 16T will be priced at INR 29,999 ($309) with 6GB of RAM and 128GB of storage, INR 31,999 ($330) with 8GB of RAM and 128GB of storage, and INR 33,999 ($351) with 8GB of RAM and 256GB of storage. Realme 16T live image (left) and box (right) The phone allegedly has an LCD screen,...

title: Google launches Gemini 3.5 Flash and Gemini Omni AI models
description: Today at its I/O conference, Google unveiled Gemini 3.5, its latest family of AI models, along with Gemini Omni, a new model that can create video from any input. The first available model in the Gemini 3.5 family is Gemini 3.5 Flash. This is now out for everyone via the Gemini app and it's also available in AI Mode in Google Search. This model "delivers intelligence that rivals large flagship models on multiple dimensions, at the speeds you have come to expect from the Flash series", Google says. It's the strongest agentic and coding Gemini model, outperforming even Gemini 3.1 Pro...

title: Infinix Hot 70 series to debut next week with a color-changing rear panel
description: Infinix recently began teasing its upcoming Infinix Hot 70 series, which is confirmed to feature a color-changing rear panel along with integrated RGB lighting. The company has also officially confirmed the launch date for the smartphones. The Infinix Hot 70 series will go official in global markets on May 25, however, we’re not sure how many models will be included in the lineup. Last year’s Hot 60 series consisted of four variants. As per the teasers shared by Infinix, the upcoming Hot 70 series will include an RGB halo lighting system at the back, along with a color-changing...

title: Google reinvents the search bar for the AI era
description: Today at its I/O conference Google has announced the biggest upgrade to the search box in over 25 years. And yes, of course - it's AI-powered now, what were you expecting? One year after its debut AI Mode has over one billion monthly users, and now Google Search is upgraded with the Gemini 3.5 Flash model, also just announced at I/O. It's the new default model for everyone globally. To complement its arrival, Google has reimagined the search box with AI. The company says "it's more intuitive than ever, dynamically expanding to give you space to describe exactly what you...

title: Xiaomi 17T series is officially coming to India
description: Yesterday, Xiaomi officially announced the launch date for the 17T and 17T Pro: May 28. Today, the company's arm in India has started teasing the fact that the series will also be available in that market. That's interesting since it's been years since a T series Xiaomi device was officially launched in India. But at least one of these two will. Xiaomi hasn't confirmed whether both the 17T and the 17T Pro will be offered in the country, and there's plenty of speculation online saying that only the cheaper model will make the cut. Capture moments the way they’re meant to be seen. Closer,...

title: Itel A100 Pro goes official with a 6.6-inch display and a 5,000mAh battery
description: Itel has launched a new budget phone in India under its A100 series, called the Itel A100 Pro. The handset features a 6.6-inch display, a 5,000mAh battery, and an orange color option that bears a striking resemblance to the iPhone 17 Pro. The Itel A100 Pro is powered by the octa-core Unisoc T7100 chipset, paired with 3GB of RAM and 64GB of internal storage. Luckily, the storage can be expanded further via a microSD card. On the front, the device sports a 6.6-inch LCD display with HD+ resolution and a 90Hz refresh rate. The panel features relatively thick bezels and a waterdrop-style...

title: Here's when the RedMagic 11S Pro is launching internationally
description: Yesterday, RedMagic unveiled the 11S Pro and 11S Pro+ gaming smartphones for the Chinese market, and in light of that you may have been wondering when to expect an international launch. RedMagic is usually quite quick to bring its devices to global markets, and the situation is similar in this case. The brand has just revealed that the global launch will be taking place on May 27 at 8 AM EDT, which is 1 PM in London, 2 PM in Central Europe, 3 PM in Eastern Europe, and 5:30 PM in India. It appears as if only one device is making the international trip. This will be called RedMagic...

title: Honor Power3 aims to break the mainstream smartphone battery capacity record
description: The Honor Power2 launched back in January with a massive 10,080 mAh battery, but the brand isn't resting on its laurels - far from it, in fact. According to a new rumor from China today, Honor is already working on the Power3, and this one may break the mainstream smartphone battery capacity record. Honor allegedly aims for its battery capacity to be 11,000-12,000 mAh, but the exact number hasn't been decided on just yet. The phone will be powered by the upcoming MediaTek Dimensity 8600 SoC. Honor Power2 The Power3 will have a 6.8-inch flat LTPS OLED screen with "1.5K" resolution....
